potential_reason: Mark Hill is a 56 MW onshore wind farm in South Ayrshire, SW Scotland, in the heavily transmission-constrained zone south of the B6 (Scotland-England) boundary. Sustained summer output far below the farm's own power curve (q50r=0.00) over ~4 months is the classic signature of Balancing Mechanism curtailment, where National Grid ESO/NESO pays Scottish wind to switch off because the B6 link cannot carry the power. SW Scotland farms (Whitelee, Clyde, etc.) are repeatedly named as the largest constraint-payment recipients.

evidence_note: No Mark Hill-specific curtailment source retrieved; inferred from location in the documented B6-constrained SW Scotland cluster.
